work_unit: EC-017 redo: --bg process group leak fix (setsid + trap delay)
situation: EC-017 original fix was in opt-engine-shift-1782940353 (rejected due to conflict with EC-007 merge in autonomous-loop.sh same region). The --bg mode had two problems: (1) nohup without setsid left child processes in the launching terminal process group, so terminal close or kill could leak orphans or miss children; (2) trap with kill 0 installed before --bg exit meant the short-lived launcher process had a trap that would kill its own terminal process group.
action: Moved trap handler to after --bg exit block (so only the long-running worker installs it). Wrapped nohup in setsid so the re-exec child becomes a new session leader with its own process group. Added detailed comments explaining the isolation.
outcome_evidence: TEST1: setsid binary found. TEST2: worker session ID (117969) differs from launcher session ID (117953), confirming process group isolation. TEST3: kill -TERM on worker PID kills both worker and its child (sleep 60), confirming trap kill 0 works within isolated group. bash -n syntax check passes.
next_suggestion: Merge ec-017-redo after human review. Then redo M-001/M-002 patches.
